label: SOC Analyst
description: Monitors security events, investigates alerts, and responds to incidents in a security operations center. Typically a Tier 1-3 progression from alert triage to threat hunting and incident response.

paths.name: Government / DoD Track
paths.rationale: Maximizes DoD 8140 approved credentials. Essential for government, military, and cleared contractor SOC positions where compliance-approved certs are mandatory. Every cert in this path is DoD 8140 approved and covers the DCWF work roles relevant to cyber defense analysis.
pros: DoD 8140 compliant throughout
Satisfies FISMA and contract requirements
High demand in cleared SOC roles
Security+ alone opens most entry-level government positions
cons: Theory-heavy certs with less practical validation than commercial alternatives
Less respected in private sector than BTL1 or GCIH
Higher overall cost than budget track

knowledge: Basic security concepts — CIA triad, common attack types
Familiarity with at least one OS at command line level
prerequisites.experience: No prior security experience required. IT helpdesk or networking experience is advantageous but not mandatory.

cert_sequence.notes: Start here — the universal entry-level gate for government and contractor roles. Study with Jason Dion's course on Udemy or Professor Messer's free materials. Highly crammable if you have IT background. Don't overthink it — pass it, move on.

Natural DoD-approved step up from Security+. Covers threat detection, vulnerability management, and incident response from a SOC analyst perspective. Performance-based questions require some hands-on practice — use CertMaster Labs or TryHackMe's SOC Level 1 path alongside study.

The most recognized IR credential and the DoD-approved capstone for this track. Open-book exam — build thorough, well-indexed notes. SANS training is expensive; community index notes and practice tests are available. Passing GCIH alongside Security+ satisfies the majority of cleared SOC Analyst job requirements.

paths.name: Practical Skills Track
paths.rationale: Maximizes hands-on skill validation with practical exams. Best for commercial SOC roles where employers value demonstrated ability over compliance checkboxes. Every cert in this path requires actual investigation and analysis work — no multiple choice.
pros: 100% practical exams prove you can do actual SOC work
Strong community recognition — BTL1 is increasingly required in UK/EU
Better preparation for real Tier 1 and Tier 2 analyst work
GCIH adds recognised vendor-neutral IR credential at the end
cons: Less DoD recognition — not suitable for cleared positions
GCIH cost is significant without employer sponsorship

knowledge: Basic security concepts — CIA triad, common attack types
prerequisites.experience: No prior security experience required. BTL1 is designed as a genuine entry point.

cert_sequence.notes: The best entry-level blue team cert on the market. 24-hour practical exam covering phishing analysis, digital forensics, SIEM investigation, and network analysis. Study the Security Blue Team course material thoroughly — the exam requires hands-on tool operation, not memorization. Use TryHackMe's SOC Level 1 path and Blue Team Labs Online for practice.

HTB's SOC analyst certification covering the full Tier 1 and Tier 2 analyst workflow in a 100% practical exam. Strong complement to BTL1 — where BTL1 is broader and foundational, CDSA goes deeper on detection engineering, SIEM, and threat hunting. Work through HTB Academy's SOC Analyst job role path before sitting the exam.

Adds the most recognized incident response credential to your practical foundation. Opens senior SOC and IR roles that require a GIAC cert by name. Open-book — build indexed notes. Community GCIH prep materials are widely available. With BTL1 and CDSA already completed, you'll have the practical foundation to genuinely understand the GCIH content rather than just memorize it.

paths.name: Budget Track
paths.rationale: Best value path for self-funded students. All certs include training, never expire, and use 100% practical exam formats. Gets you job-ready for commercial Tier 1 SOC roles at under $1,000 total investment.
pros: Lowest total cost of any SOC path — under $1,000 including training
All exams are 100% practical — no MCQ cramming
Neither cert expires — no renewal fees
Demonstrates real skill rather than exam performance
cons: Not DoD 8140 approved — unsuitable for cleared positions
Less name recognition than GIAC in large enterprise environments
No GCIH means missing the most recognized IR credential at senior level

prerequisites.experience: No prior security experience required. This is explicitly designed as a zero-to-employed path.

cert_sequence.notes: Start here. 24-hour practical exam, $500 including training, never expires. The single best value entry-level SOC credential available. Recognized enough to land first SOC jobs — particularly in UK, Europe, and at companies that value practical skill over brand names. Use Blue Team Labs Online free tier and TryHackMe for additional practice.

TCM Security's SOC analyst cert at $249 including training. 100% practical exam with human-reviewed report — not flags. Complements BTL1 well since it approaches SOC analysis from a slightly different angle with stronger emphasis on report writing. The two together demonstrate practical breadth that many hiring managers find more convincing than a single MCQ cert. Never expires.

paths.name: Microsoft Defender Track
paths.rationale: For SOC analysts working in Microsoft-dominant environments — the most common enterprise stack in large organizations. SC-200 is increasingly required for analyst roles at Microsoft-tooled SOCs. Paired with CySA+ for DoD recognition and broad threat analysis coverage.
pros: SC-200 required by name at most Microsoft-centric SOC roles
CySA+ adds DoD 8140 recognition alongside Microsoft credentials
Low per-cert cost relative to GIAC alternatives
Directly applicable to Sentinel, Defender XDR, and Microsoft 365 Defender operations

prerequisites.experience: Any IT operations or helpdesk background is sufficient to start this path.

cert_sequence.notes: Microsoft Security, Compliance, and Identity Fundamentals — the entry-level Microsoft security credential. Very accessible, cheap ($165), and provides the Microsoft security ecosystem overview needed before SC-200. Skip if you already have SC-200 level knowledge.

Microsoft Security Operations Analyst — the primary credential for Sentinel and Defender XDR operations. KQL is the most tested skill — practice daily in Microsoft's free Sentinel learning environment. Microsoft Learn SC-200 path is comprehensive free preparation.

CompTIA CySA+ adds the DoD 8140 approved threat analysis credential. Covers threat hunting methodology, vulnerability management, and incident response at an analyst level. With SC-200's Microsoft-specific operations foundation, CySA+ adds the vendor-neutral analytical depth and DoD recognition.

paths.name: CrowdStrike / Falcon Track
paths.rationale: For SOC analysts working in CrowdStrike Falcon-deployed environments — the dominant EDR platform. The Falcon certification stack (Administrator → Responder → Hunter) directly validates operational capability on the most widely deployed enterprise EDR. Highly valued at CrowdStrike-deployed organizations where these certs are required by name.
pros: CrowdStrike certs required by name at Falcon-deployed organizations
Covers the full SOC operational workflow — admin, response, and hunting — on a single platform
CCFH (Falcon Hunter) validates advanced threat hunting capability
CrowdStrike Falcon is the dominant enterprise EDR — skills in high demand
cons: Requires CrowdStrike Falcon access for training and exam preparation
Less recognized at organizations running different EDR platforms

prerequisites.experience: Security+ or equivalent foundation recommended. CrowdStrike Falcon access (trial or employer-provided) is essential for preparation.

cert_sequence.notes: Security foundation and DoD baseline before platform specialization. Provides the security concepts context that makes CrowdStrike's detection logic comprehensible.

CrowdStrike Certified Falcon Administrator — covers Falcon platform administration, sensor deployment, policy configuration, and basic detection management. The prerequisite for CCFR. Falcon-specific training is required — access through CrowdStrike University.

CrowdStrike Certified Falcon Responder — covers investigating detections, real-time response, and incident investigation in Falcon. The core SOC analyst credential for CrowdStrike environments. Validates the day-to-day alert triage and investigation workflow.

CrowdStrike Certified Falcon Hunter — the advanced threat hunting credential covering custom detection development, behavioral hunting queries in Falcon, and proactive threat hunting methodology within the CrowdStrike ecosystem. The senior SOC analyst credential in CrowdStrike environments.

paths.name: Palo Alto XDR Track
paths.rationale: For SOC analysts in Palo Alto Cortex XSIEM/XDR environments. The Palo Alto SecOps certification stack covers Cortex XDR analysis and XSOAR automation — increasingly common at enterprise and government organizations deploying Palo Alto's security operations platform.
pros: Directly applicable to Cortex XDR and XSOAR operations
PANW SecOps Professional broadly covers the Palo Alto security operations ecosystem
Palo Alto is rapidly growing in enterprise SOC deployments
XSOAR automation skills are high-value and underrepresented
cons: Less recognized at non-Palo Alto tooled organizations
Palo Alto SOC cert market is newer and still developing

prerequisites.experience: Security operations background recommended. Palo Alto Beacon free training provides foundational knowledge before formal certification.

cert_sequence.notes: Security foundation before Palo Alto platform specialization.

Palo Alto Networks Certified XDR Analyst — covers Cortex XDR detection, investigation, and response workflows. The entry point for Palo Alto SOC operations. Complete Palo Alto's free Beacon training path before attempting.

Palo Alto Networks Certified XSOAR Engineer — covers XSOAR playbook development, automation, and SOC orchestration. High-value skill as SOAR adoption accelerates. Engineers who can build XSOAR automation are in significantly shorter supply than those who can just operate it.

Palo Alto Networks Certified Security Operations Professional — the capstone Palo Alto SOC credential covering the full Cortex ecosystem. With XDR Analyst and XSOAR Engineer completed, this validates comprehensive Palo Alto SOC platform mastery.
